title: Budgets, Interest, Loans, and Financial Decisions · Decision Notebook: Loan Total Cost
summary: Compare household plans, emergency savings, credit costs, and loan offers without hiding assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg loan total cost increment in an explainable household decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare payment, total repayment, term, fees, and opportunity cost across loan offers.
Model compound growth by period and distinguish nominal from effective change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Loan Total Cost evidence
steps.instruction: $1300 grows once per year at 5.5% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value. Use the result to decide Loan Total Cost for an explainable household decision model.
steps.why: The Loan Total Cost result for an explainable household decision model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1300, r=0.055, and t=2. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare payment, total repayment, term, fees, and opportunity cost across loan offers.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Loan Total Cost, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for an explainable household decision model. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare payment, total repayment, term, fees, and opportunity cost across loan offers.

hints: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1300, r=0.055, and t=2.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.
